title: Relay channel closure treated as disconnect, not error
description:
GOOD CHOICE: When the relay event channel closes (None case), this is treated as a clean exit, not a fatal error (event.rs 43-50). The client sets connection state to Offline and displays a system message, then the loop continues until state.quit is set.

A contributor might treat None as a fatal error and break the loop or propagate it as RelayMessage::Error to other clients. But that would be wrong: the relay connection manager handles reconnection asynchronously; a closed channel just means this select branch is done.

This is non-obvious but essential for the reconnection semantics where the client event loop continues even when temporarily offline.
close_reason: Verified invariant holds in current code; documented in DESIGN.md §14 TUI terminal safety invariants
